issues.title: State [*] start/end pseudo-state markers lose their clean semantic look in the interactive/exported-HTML sketch view (parity gap vs the static SVG/PNG renderer)
issues.description: The interactive runtime (flowchart runtime reused for state) rough-drew every node uniformly, including the two pseudo-state circles, so start/end became near-identical scribbled blobs in the live view AND the exported Save-SVG/PNG — losing the solid-dot (start) vs ringed-circle (end) distinction the static state SVG preserves.
issues.proposed_solution: Tag the pseudo-state nodes so the interactive/exported sketch renderer draws the same clean solid-dot / ringed-circle markers as the static renderer.
issues.fix_summary: Added an optional PositionedNode.stateMarker ('start'|'end'), set by layoutState on the shrunk pseudo-state nodes (rides the model → serialized payload with zero serialization change). The DOM runtime, in SKETCH mode only, renders those nodes as clean <circle> markers (solid dot for start; ring + inner dot for end) in both the live view and toSvgString() — skipping the rough treatment — mirroring src/native/state/svg.ts. Gated to sketch (no clean-mode change, no existing state-e2e risk). Verified: the tester's own e2e regression (e2e/sketch.spec.ts, previously RED) now PASSES; added a fast unit guard in dom-runtime-parity.test.ts; full suite green (unit 339, e2e 73).

issues.title: Library API silently ignores an invalid `style` value (falls back to clean, no error/warning) — inconsistent with the CLI's explicit validation
issues.description: renderSvg/renderSvgAsync/mount et al only check `style === 'sketch'`; any other value (e.g. a typo'd 'Sketch' from an untyped JS caller) silently renders clean, while the CLI hard-errors on `--style bogus`. TypeScript's RenderStyle union guards compile-time-checked callers.
issues.proposed_solution: Validate once at a shared entry point (throw a RangeError or console.warn + fall back to clean), OR accept given the typed surface.
issues.fix_summary: ACCEPTED (works-as-designed). `RenderStyle` is a compile-time union, so the library's intended TS consumers are guarded at build time; the CLI validates because it converts an untyped string arg (the actual untyped boundary). Unlike REV-003 (a valid value inapplicable to a runtime-detected diagram type — uncatchable at compile time, hence a runtime warn), an invalid style literal IS compile-catchable, so runtime-validating every library entry point is disproportionate for a P3 with no functional impact. Documented; a defensive `normalizeStyle` warn remains an easy follow-up if desired.
